initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of left ventricular hypertrophy?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose left ventricular hypertrophy, which of the following voltage criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of low voltage QRS complexes (< 0.5mV) in limb leads
R wave amplitude in lead aVL > 1.1mV
Sum of R wave in lead I and S wave in lead III > 2.5mV
Presence of pathological Q waves in inferior leads
criterion_selection.answer: R wave amplitude in lead aVL > 1.1mV

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is the maximum R amplitude in lead aVL greater than 1.1mV? Note that R amplitude is the amplitude of the positive deflection in the QRS complex, measured from the baseline to the highest point of the QRS complex.

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the R amplitude in lead aVL? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured R amplitude fall into?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have left ventricular hypertrophy? Note that all standard voltage criteria should be considered before making a final diagnosis of left ventricular hypertrophy.
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the voltage criterion you just evaluated, which other voltage criterion should be evaluated to diagnose left ventricular hypertrophy?
options: Sum of R amplitude in V2 and S amplitude in V4 > 4.0mV
Sum of S amplitude in V1 and S amplitude in V5 > 3.0mV
Sum of S amplitude in V1 and R amplitude in V5/V6 > 3.5mV
Presence of pathological Q waves in inferior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Sum of S amplitude in V1 and R amplitude in V5/V6 > 3.5mV

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is the sum of the maximum S amplitude in lead V1 and the maximum R amplitude in lead V5 or V6 greater than 3.5mV? Note that S amplitude is the amplitude of the negative deflection in the QRS complex, measured from the baseline to the lowest point of the QRS complex.

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the S amplitude in lead V1? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured S amplitude fall into?
Which of the following leads show the larger R amplitude? Select the lead from the options below.
In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the R amplitude in the selected lead? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured R amplitude fall into?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have left ventricular hypertrophy? Note that all standard voltage criteria should be considered before making a final diagnosis of left ventricular hypertrophy.
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the voltage criteria you have evaluated so far, which other voltage criterion should be evaluated to diagnose left ventricular hypertrophy?
options: Presence of pathological Q waves in inferior leads
Presence of low voltage QRS complexes (< 0.5mV) in limb leads
Ratio of R wave to S wave amplitude in lead V1 > 1.0
Cornell Product ((R amplitude in aVL + S amplitude in V3) x QRS duration) > 0.244mV*s
Sum of R wave in V1 and S wave in V5 > 1.1mV
criterion_selection.answer: Cornell Product ((R amplitude in aVL + S amplitude in V3) x QRS duration) > 0.244mV*s

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is the Cornell Product calculated on this ECG greater than 0.244mV*s? Note that the Cornell Product is calculated as the sum of the S amplitude in lead V3 and the R amplitude in lead aVL, multiplied by the QRS duration in seconds.

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the S amplitude in lead V3? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured S amplitude fall into?
In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the R amplitude in lead aVL? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured R amplitude fall into?
For the selected segment, which range does the measured QRS duration fall into?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have left ventricular hypertrophy? Note that all standard voltage criteria should be considered before making a final diagnosis of left ventricular hypertrophy.
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: Since only one voltage criterion was met, which specific non-voltage criterion should be evaluated to diagnose left ventricular hypertrophy?
options: Presence of an LV strain pattern in lateral leads
Presence of pathological Q waves in inferior leads
Presence of hyperacute T waves in leads V5 and V6
Presence of tall, peaked T waves in lateral leads
Presence of diffuse ST elevation in lateral leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of an LV strain pattern in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show an LV strain, characterized by ST depression with T wave inversion, in any of the lateral leads (I, aVL, V5, and V6)?

grounding.question: To verify that the lateral leads do not show an LV strain pattern, in which of the following segments can you locate the ST segment and T wave in these leads? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this ECG suggest the presence of left ventricular hypertrophy?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
